_source: Kubernetes documentation, SRE workbooks, Production incident post-mortems
_note: Long-tail DevOps/SRE knowledge. K8s debugging, observability, incident response, platform reliability.

pending.meaning: Pod accepted but not running yet
pending.debug: kubectl describe pod <name> | Look for Events section; check Conditions; verify resource requests vs node capacity

crashloopbackoff.meaning: Container exits shortly after starting; k8s restarts with exponential backoff
crashloopbackoff.debug: kubectl logs <pod> --previous; check exit code; increase initialDelaySeconds on probes; add init containers for dependencies

imagepullbackoff.meaning: Cannot pull container image from registry
imagepullbackoff.debug: kubectl describe pod; check 'Failed to pull image' message; verify image exists: docker pull <image>; create imagePullSecret if needed

oomkilled.meaning: Pod exceeded memory limit; kernel OOM killer terminated it
oomkilled.prevention: Set appropriate memory limits; monitor actual usage; add memory requests = limits for guaranteed QoS
oomkilled.debug: kubectl describe pod | grep -A5 'Last State'; see exit code 137; check metrics-server: kubectl top pod

pod_to_pod_fails.check_cni: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep -E 'calico|flannel|weave'; CNI plugin must be running
pod_to_pod_fails.check_network_policy: kubectl get networkpolicy -A; policies can block pod-to-pod traffic; describe and review ingress/egress rules

service_not_reachable.check_endpoints: kubectl get endpoints <service-name>; should list pod IPs; empty = label selector mismatch
service_not_reachable.label_mismatch: kubectl get pods --show-labels; compare to service selector: kubectl get svc <name> -o yaml | grep selector
service_not_reachable.port_mismatch: Service targetPort must match container containerPort; common mistake: targetPort=80 but app listens on 8080

ingress_not_working.ingress_controller: kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx (or traefik, etc.); controller must be running
ingress_not_working.ingress_class: Check ingressClassName matches controller; missing = default controller; multiple controllers need explicit class
ingress_not_working.dns_propagation: curl -H 'Host: example.com' <ingress-ip>; bypasses DNS; if works, DNS issue; if fails, ingress config issue
ingress_not_working.tls_issues: Verify cert secret exists in same namespace; check CN/SAN matches hostname; cert-manager logs for ACME failures

requests_vs_limits.requests: Guaranteed resources; kube-scheduler uses for placement; pod only scheduled if node has requests available
requests_vs_limits.limits: Maximum resources; pod throttled (CPU) or OOMKilled (memory) if exceeded; limits > requests = burstable

qos_classes.guaranteed: requests = limits for all containers; highest priority; last to be evicted
qos_classes.burstable: requests < limits or only requests set; medium priority
qos_classes.besteffort: No requests or limits; lowest priority; first to be evicted under pressure

recommendations.cpu: Set requests based on p50 usage; limits 2-4x requests (allow bursts); no limits for batch jobs
recommendations.memory: Set requests based on p90; limits = requests (memory not compressible); exceeding limit = OOMKill

hpa_horizontal_pod_autoscaling.metrics_source: Requires metrics-server; install: kubectl apply -f metrics-server.yaml
hpa_horizontal_pod_autoscaling.custom_metrics: Prometheus adapter; scale on requests/sec, queue depth, etc.; more accurate than CPU

troubleshooting.hpa_not_scaling: kubectl get hpa; check TARGETS column shows <unknown>, means metrics-server not installed or pod has no resource requests
troubleshooting.flapping: HPA scales up/down rapidly; increase stabilizationWindowSeconds to 300; adjust targetUtilization threshold

cluster_autoscaling.node_autoscaler: Adds nodes when pods pending due to insufficient resources; removes underutilized nodes
cluster_autoscaling.limitations: Only works with cloud providers (GKE, EKS, AKS); 5-10 minute delay to add node; pods with local storage or anti-affinity may block scale-down
cluster_autoscaling.pod_disruption_budget: Prevents autoscaler from draining node if would violate PDB; ensure PDB allows some disruption (maxUnavailable=1)

pvc_states.pending: PVC waiting for PV; dynamic provisioning may be creating; or no PV matches claim
pvc_states.bound: PVC matched to PV; pod can use volume
pvc_states.lost: PV deleted but PVC still references it; data likely lost

storage_classes.dynamic_provisioning: StorageClass with provisioner; PVC automatically creates PV; cloud-specific (gp2, pd-ssd, etc.)

reclaim_policy.delete: PV and backing storage deleted when PVC deleted; default; dangerous for prod data
reclaim_policy.retain: PV marked released but not deleted; manual cleanup required; safe for prod

volume_binding_mode.immediate: PV created immediately; may be in wrong zone for pod
volume_binding_mode.waitforfirstconsumer: PV created when pod scheduled; ensures PV in same zone as pod; recommended

troubleshooting.mount_failed: kubectl describe pod; check Events; common: PV filesystem type mismatch, PV in different AZ than node, SELinux blocking mount
troubleshooting.read_only_filesystem: PV mounted read-only; check fsGroup and runAsUser in securityContext; may need initContainer to chown
troubleshooting.performance_issues: Use storage class with SSD backing (gp3, pd-ssd); check IOPS limits; consider local-path-provisioner for high-IOPS needs

logging.kubectl_logs: kubectl logs <pod> -f --tail=100; -c <container> for multi-container; --previous for crashed container
logging.centralized_logging: Fluentd/Fluent Bit + Elasticsearch + Kibana; or Loki + Grafana; collects logs from all pods
logging.structured_logging: Log JSON; easier to parse and search; include request_id, user_id, trace_id for correlation

metrics.prometheus: De facto standard; scrapes /metrics endpoint; long-term storage with Thanos or Cortex

key_metrics.red_method: Rate, Errors, Duration; for request-driven services
key_metrics.use_method: Utilization, Saturation, Errors; for resources (CPU, disk, network)

metrics.recording_rules: Pre-compute expensive queries; rate(http_requests_total[5m]); reduces dashboard load time
metrics.alerting_rules: Alert on symptoms, not causes; 'API latency p99 > 1s' not 'CPU > 80%'; use for loop to avoid flapping

tracing.jaeger_zipkin: Distributed tracing; follows request through microservices; identifies slow services
tracing.instrumentation: OpenTelemetry SDK; auto-instruments frameworks (Flask, Express, Spring); adds trace_id to logs
tracing.sampling: Trace 1-10% of requests; head-based (sample before decision) or tail-based (sample slow requests)

rbac.roles_vs_clusterroles: Role = namespace-scoped; ClusterRole = cluster-wide (nodes, PVs) or namespace-agnostic
rbac.debugging: kubectl auth can-i <verb> <resource> --as=<user>; check if user can perform action
rbac.principle_of_least_privilege: Grant minimum permissions; avoid cluster-admin except for admins; use namespaced roles

network_policies.default_deny: Create 'deny all' policy; then whitelist specific traffic; zero-trust model
network_policies.ingress_egress: Ingress = incoming to pod; Egress = outgoing from pod; both can be restricted
network_policies.example_allow_from_label: Allow pods with label app=frontend to access app=backend on port 8080; blocks all other traffic
network_policies.testing: kubectl exec into pod; curl other service; should be blocked if policy working; check policy with kubectl describe networkpolicy

security_context.drop_capabilities: Drop ALL, add only needed (NET_BIND_SERVICE for port <1024)

pod_security.pod_security_standards: Privileged, Baseline, Restricted; enforce with Pod Security Admission (PSA); replaces PodSecurityPolicy
pod_security.seccomp_apparmor: Restrict syscalls; prevent container breakout; default seccomp profile blocks ~300 dangerous syscalls

secrets_management.kubernetes_secrets: Base64 encoded NOT encrypted; etcd encryption at rest must be enabled
secrets_management.external_secrets: External Secrets Operator; sync from Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager; secrets never stored in etcd
secrets_management.sealed_secrets: Encrypt secrets in Git; controller decrypts in-cluster; gitops-friendly

backup.etcd_backup: etcdctl snapshot save; backs up all cluster state; critical for disaster recovery
backup.velero: Backs up k8s resources and PVs; supports scheduled backups; cloud-specific volume snapshots
backup.frequency: etcd: daily; Velero: daily + before cluster upgrades

ha_considerations.control_plane: 3 or 5 master nodes (odd number for quorum); across availability zones; load balancer for API server
ha_considerations.worker_nodes: Spread across AZs; pod anti-affinity for replicas; PDBs to prevent full service outage during node drain
ha_considerations.multi_cluster: Federation or service mesh (Istio multi-cluster); failover between clusters; complexity vs. availability tradeoff

performance_tuning.etcd: Use SSD; 3 or 5 node cluster (not 4 or 6); separate etcd from master for large clusters; defrag regularly
performance_tuning.dns: CoreDNS autoscaling; increase replicas for large clusters; pod antiaffinity; local DNS cache (NodeLocal DNSCache)
